LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ARTHUR J.GALLAGER & CO. INSURANCE BROKERS' OF DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. THE EXECUTIVE AGREEMENT DOES NOT ADDRESS ALL THE TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT. THE TERMS IN PARAGRAPH 12 ARE NOT FREE FROM AMBIGUITY; THE ALLEGED FAILURE TO PAY SEVERANCE IS A SUFFICIENT BASIS TO ALLEGE A BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT; FRAUD HAS BEEN PLED IN THAT PLAINTIFF WAS INDUCED TO LEAVE HIS PRIOR JOB BASED ON DEFENDANT'S PROMISES UPON WHICH HE REASONABLY RELIED. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
